---
title: "Get started"
subtitle: "Read an image, validate its spatial contract, and extract an ROI time series."
jupyter: python3
---

## Install

```bash
pip install neuroim
```

For a source checkout:

```bash
pip install -e .
```

## The 30-second tour

Read a small 4D image and a matching mask.

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parent / "src"))

import numpy as np
import neuroim as ni

repo = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "docs" else Path.cwd()

bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol")

bold.shape, mask.shape, bold.space.compatible_with(mask.space)
```

Define a spherical region of interest and extract its time-by-voxel matrix.

```{python}
roi = ni.spherical_roi(mask, centroid=(4, 4, 2), radius=2)
time_by_voxel = bold.series_roi(roi)

time_by_voxel.shape
```

Project the ROI summary back into the same mask space and hand it to nibabel-compatible tooling.

```{python}
mean_data = np.zeros(mask.shape, dtype=np.float32)
mean_data[tuple(roi.coords.T)] = time_by_voxel.mean(axis=0)

mean_map = ni.NeuroVol.from_array(mean_data, space=mask.space)
out = mean_map.to_nibabel()

out.shape, out.affine.tolist()
```

## What just happened

`read_image()` returns typed image containers with `NeuroSpace` attached. The BOLD series and mask can be checked for spatial compatibility before ROI extraction. `spherical_roi()` returns voxel coordinates in that space, `series_roi()` returns an analysis-ready time-by-voxel matrix, and `NeuroVol.from_array()` turns derived arrays back into spatially grounded maps.

## Interoperability with nibabel

Use the explicit interop methods when code already holds a nibabel image.

```{python}
import nibabel as nib

nib_img = nib.load(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz"))
vec = ni.NeuroVec.from_nibabel(nib_img)
round_trip = vec.to_nibabel()

vec.shape, round_trip.shape
```

## Next steps

- [Tutorial: Core objects](tutorials/core-objects.qmd)
- [API reference](reference/index.qmd)
